    Scrutiny turns to safety set-up of Swiss bar after deadly blaze

    Witnesses reported seeing staff at the Le Constellation bar carrying so-called fountain candles atop bottles of champagne, and questions have also arisen about a foam material used to soundproof the ceiling of the basement where revellers danced.

    Swiss resort fire likely started by sparkler candles

    'Fountain candles' were carried aloft too close to the ceiling, a local prosecutor said.

Breakingviews Predictions

  • Why investors will learn to love government bonds

    Global state debt has hit $100 trln, and borrowing costs have wobbled for Britain, France and the US. But if inflation keeps cooling and central banks cut rates, 2026 may be different. Bond investors could become relatively care-free buyers again, bringing yields lower.

    Jon Sindreu

Homepage USJanuary 3, 2026 · 2:16 AM GMT+7

Survivor recounts 'miraculous' escape from deadly Swiss bar blaze

A young survivor, Axel, recounted on Friday (January 2) shielding himself with a table, and breaking a window to escape from the inferno at a bar in Switzerland where dozens of revelers died in a blaze.

Information you can trust

Reuters, the news and media division of Thomson Reuters, is the world’s largest multimedia news provider, reaching billions of people worldwide every day. Reuters provides business, financial, national and international news to professionals via desktop terminals, the world's media organizations, industry events and directly to consumers.
